channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: ICP — 30 replies — 18.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
ICP

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-06-08 23:54 UTC

OP

>we have steve jobs at home

Early replies (first 3)

62336952
>>62336934
soon

62337106
>>62336934
ICP has the best tech

62337150
>>62337106
ICUP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62337165
>>62337150
ICPDD8U using baby oil

62337236
>>62337106
You can have the best tech around but if you have an incomptent parasite at the helm, it doesn't matter when all he does is extract

62337255
>>62336934
Dom is next Bill Gates but not a freak. ICP is new paradigm (yes I know meme word but it is unironically true) on same level as the PC

62337283
>>62337255
It's funny you say cause Bill gates is just a parasite who sucks off value and gives people aids, yeah you may be right, he is the next rat gates

62337316
>>62337236
I remember in 2000s when Amazon was down 99% in stocks while they were fully operational and people just like you tried to demoralize investors.

62337352
>>62337255
Bill Gates fucked a Russian hooker. Honestly based

62337356
>>62337316
It's been 6 years, faggot, 6 years and nothing to show for it, amazon gave back some profits post crash after 3 years, nobody is profitable on this piece of shit

62337365
>>62337356
>nothing to show for it

1. ICP was launched 5 years ago not 5
2. Caffeine.AI subscriptions are up 30% this month.
3 Partnerships with the UN, Switzerland, Pakistan, HBAR, Maestro etc..
4. Chain fusion

62337372
>>62337356
UN

62337376
>>62337316
I am ICP Chad but let’s not lie. Amazon went down ~90% after Dotcom crash and only once. ICP has gone down ~90% many times over (scam pump followed by crash) and is now down ~99% from ath. ICP is being repressed 10x more than Amazon because it is 10x a bigger deal

62337385
>>62337376
That’s because you’re ill informed. $700 was never the ATH, it was more like $80.
Hunchbacks used futures to manipulate the price at launch. That $700 literally lasted for a few hours before they deliberately crashed it.

62337420
>>62337376
Forget back -90%, nobody profited from this since Moonman shilled this around $50, the last semi good pump was from $3 to $20, that was the last pump, the somehow tech couldn't keep any pump because the rat of switzerland just keeps extracting, OGs were raped, early adopters never got anything back

62337568
>>62337420
Your obsession with moonman is actually a mental illness.
ICP is now far more advanced and mature than it was in 2024 when we reached almost $20.
The next pump will be much more extreme because ICP has had many adoptions this year.
You need to stop fudding, it’s really not healthy for you.

62337596
>>62337420
Also, mission 70 already cut inflation by 40%. The tokenomics are much better now than when ICP was worth $50. We just need an alt season to get this party going.

62337713
>>62337372
>icy pee is partnered with the foremost advocate of white genocide
bearish

62337760
>>62336934
I am considering dumping 10k into this. It might fail spectacularly but it’s the lowest MC and highest potential coin I can think of. I am tired of working myself to death.

62337787
>>62336934
Dis-lui que je lui passe le bonjour

62337830
>>62337760
That’s a good mindset. Worst case scenario you lose a few thousand dollars or you make half a million when the AI alt season starts.

62337832
>>62337385
I understand the futures Sam Barkman scam. I was going off a $200 top. Even $80 top puts ICP down 97% however

62337836
>>62337760
10k is a good gamble for something that has potential to retire you, just don’t over extend because the risk is big. ICP is imo still the most exciting narrative besides chainlink in crypto right now

62337916
>>62337836
Chainlink is not that exciting lol

62337929
>>62336934
Lol the black turtleneck lives on

62337937
>>62337760
For your own good, don't, trust me, i've been suckered into this scam for 6 years, it has fundamental issues stemming from it's core team, they are ok with the price hovering low just surviving and extracting

62337958
>>62337787
are you talking about mm?

62338026
>>62337937
I’ve held on and off for years as well. But seems like things are starting to click now.

62338066
>>62338026
Don’t listen to him, he’s a known fudder on biz, he hated ICP for years, his obsession with moonman is a mental illness.

62338597
>>62337916
Chainlink is very exciting, they research so much

item.title: Imagine panicking over 32 BTC — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Imagine panicking over 32 BTC

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-09 01:19 UTC

OP

Everyone was convinced Strategy had started dumping, then today's filing shows they added another 1,550 BTC.

That's why I wouldn't panic over every little sale. You never know if a much bigger buy is right around the corner.

Early replies (first 3)

62336455
>>62336408
Does not matter

62337260
>>62336455
Use your first to buy Bitcoin anon.

62337278
>>62336408
And raised a bunch of cash too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62338849
>>62337278
They're smart

item.title: There's ZERO way to make good money anymore — 11 replies — 13.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
There's ZERO way to make good money anymore

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-09 00:40 UTC

OP

> crypto is obviously deader than dead
> stocks are at ath's so any x's opportunity is loooooonnnnng gone
> yt is nearly impossible to stand out with how easily it is to get demonetized + hypersaturation
> other social media platforms pay pennies on the dollar compared to nayzee youtube
> "just learn a trade bro" anybody who has said this has never worked in the trades nor understands only union + business owners get those crazy salaries braindead yt comments talk about
> white collar is getting obliterated by AI
> "just learn ai bro" join the pacific ocean sized competition pool you're competing against for micro saas or whatever the fuck idea you might have
> "just invest S&P for 30 years bro" this only works if you have disposable income - please read above points for why that isn't really possible anymore if you're not already in a secure high paying jobs
> and of course, inflation will obliterate any last bit of savings you might have from your income

Why is it so flippin fucking impossible to make it these days?

Early replies (first 3)

62338538
get a job

62338547
>>62338538
as a criminal

62338553
>>62338538
mechs are possible?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62338571
>>62338438
Get a job. Any job. Stop crying.

62338582
>>62338571
>tip your supervisor
shit made me laugh

62338585
You can walk onto any job site in america and get a job that day. Shave, wear a suit, have a firm handshake and eye contact. That’s how you make money nowadays.

62338586
>>62338438
erm, CRIME???

62338635
>>62338538
>>62338571
>>62338585
Nobody can read OP posts these days? Also you clearly haven't seen today's job market with 80%+ fake jobs (and the real ones are hypercompetitive - even m7 mba's are struggling to find work). Also, those pitiful salaries won't even pay the bills, and the salaries keep dipping over the past couple years too.

62338640
>>62338635
between my job andstonks im up 130k this year so far

62338643
>>62338438
The next big thing: savings bonds
Get your Treasury Direct account now while you can

62338740
>>62338438
Stop coming up with excuses and just make money, lol

62338329

62338335

62338559

thats unfortunate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62338790

item.title: MSTR bears still haven’t figured out the most basic math on earth. — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
MSTR bears still haven’t figured out the most basic math on earth.

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-09 01:25 UTC

OP

MSTR bears still haven’t figured out the most basic math on earth. Strategy’s Bitcoin NAV all-time high was Oct. 6, 2025.
They had ~640,031 BTC.
BTC closed at $124,725.
Gross BTC NAV: ~$79.83B.
Since then, while bears were writing fan fiction about “dilution” and “Ponzi collapse,” Strategy stacked another 205,225 BTC.
They now hold 845,256 BTC.
That means Strategy does not need BTC to return to $124k+ for its Bitcoin NAV to hit a new all-time high.
It only needs BTC back to ~$94,442.

Read that again.

Strategy can break its old BTC NAV ATH with Bitcoin still ~24% BELOW the prior BTC price peak.
At $100k BTC, Strategy’s Bitcoin NAV is already ~$84.5B.
At the old $124.7k close, Strategy’s Bitcoin NAV would be ~$105.4B.
The bears spent the entire drawdown screaming about leverage while Saylor increased the stack by 32%.
They analyzed the wrapper and missed the weapon.
They mocked the machine while the machine lowered the hurdle rate for a new NAV ATH by more than $30,000 per BTC.
This is accumulation domination.

The bear case depends on pretending the numerator stopped growing.

It got 205,225 BTC bigger

Early replies (first 3)

62338853
>>62337582
And what happens if bitcoin just drops to 40k before returning to 100k?

62338863
Didnt read
Butfuck is going to zero. Worthless. Why buy bitfuck. It does nothing. Semiconductors 2x every month

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Germany Is Primed For Economic Boom — 3 replies — 6.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Germany Is Primed For Economic Boom

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-09 01:02 UTC

OP

Germany is in the middle of the biggest economic shift since the invention of the steam engine and it is over the halfway mark: Energiewende.

Now, with clean efficient renewable energy Germany has began explosive investment into AI and modern tech industries. The largest economy in Europe and the third largest in the world has entered the ring against the US tech giants. With modern energy and while the rest of the world struggles with oil shortages and the Iranian crisis (and the Trump crisis), the timing could not be better.

There is often negativity surrounding Germany, both here and broadly in the media. However, these negative sentiments are often sensationalist and poorly considered. This video explains well why Germany is still in a fantastic economic position. He does not mention it, but the increased energy independence with cheap clean renewable sources is even more beneficial for manufacturing. The growing pains are over and now the goliath can awaken.
https://youtu.be/cg43s4PkPX0

This new green economy also creates a host of growth opportunities in renewables. Both domestically but also abroad when other countries are forced to go through the process Germany has in order to modernize their energy. Fossil fuels must end we cannot continue to rely upon them or the consequences will be disaster. Hopefully, Americans can be convinced of this before they doom us all.

Which also brings us to the last issue: PPP. Based on OECD data, equivalent prices in Germany would be 81% of what they are in America. Even this data fails to capture the entire story in my opinion because it is all prices. The reality is necessary goods like healthcare, food, and education are far cheaper in Germany while consumer goods are more similar. The result not only are Germans with the same income relatively richer in all areas but the economy is more equal as only luxury goods are truly expensive because there are not allowed these corporate monopolies on the basic needs of life.

Early replies (first 3)

62338713
>>62338691
Kek nice fanfiction

62338716
>>62338691
Europeans are too lazy, overregulated, and overtaxed to grow

62338787
Germany will never be allowed to boom, as punishment for WWII. Their resources will be extracted, but they won't see a red cent.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: I got a comfy wfh job doing interviews for a political campaign company. — 9 replies — 6.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I got a comfy wfh job doing interviews for a political campaign company.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-09 00:08 UTC

OP

I got a comfy wfh job doing interviews for a political campaign company. I get paid $15 an hour but my workload is so minimal and as long as I screen the candidates properly they all get passed to the second stage.

That being said it's temporary. Ends in November.

I don't want to go back to my warehouse labor job.

What can I learn within the next 4-5 months that can save me from retail/warehouse/janitorial/hard labor/fast food?

I want a big boy job. I want big boy money. I want something REAL.

Early replies (first 3)

62334531
do you have a degree? go back to school for electrical engineering and become a verification engineer. starting is nuts, the industry is cyclical and the work is not something you can skirt by with LLMs, that's why it's safe. also it has no WFH, so you probably have to move to Arizona or some shit; but honestly that's better than the poverty hellhole cycle. Also you wont suffer from layoffs.

62334536
>>62334531
Well I already live in AZ so I guess I'm one step of the way

62334543
>>62334531
Do electrical engineers break their knees and bodies or is that just an electrician thing?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62334545
>>62334543
wow you're dumber than I thought, I don't think you can do it buddy.

62334571
>>62334531
i have a college friend that was EE and became a verification engineer and he said its really hard to get promoted in the industry. salary is decent though.

62334617
>>62334545
I knew that would trigger you which is why I said it

62337912
you should probably find another job like your current one, it sounds more promising than some warehouse job. plus you have experience you can carry over to a relevant job, not waste the potential on some forklift position.

62337919
>>62334526
Start applying to similar jobs asap
Worst case scenario you’re working two for awhile and making bank
B)
Otherwise, are you good at sales? Do you like talking to people?

62338647
>>62334526
stay in politics. that's obviously as far your witt will get you. i highly doubt you can do 4 yrs of EE uni when you act like this jeet

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 162 replies — 39.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 162 • Updated: 2026-06-08 21:20 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62325301
>>62325222
>>62324777
nice, very nice
But yes, while you can point to 5 years down 70% and be pissy, you can also say, for something with solid fundamentals, its the perfect time to buy. All a question of if crypto is legitimate or remains a scam casino.

62327001
>>62322856
I don't have to prove that at all, you specifically said the mechanism whereby token from the uncirculating supply are used to pay the nodes "decouples the link price from the usage of the network".

62327952
>>62327001
I specifically said CLL accepting payments outside of the Link token and then paying node operators with their team wallets instead of making customers pay in Link or immediately converting the payments to Link from the market decouples the usage of the network from the price.

You are trying to make the BTC mining comparison but it falls apart. You can say CLL paying from team wallets is the same as miners mining BTC, but miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block. Node operators do not decide their rates of payment, CLL does. Miners will charge to maintain operations and so BTC's price is intrinsically tied to the cost of operations. After all, no new BTC enters the market outside of miners thus the BTC's price tag will be influenced by operation costs. >>62322856's etherscan wallet proves new Link that enters the market does not go through node operators first meaning that they're stripped of influencing the price from operational costs. This is what I mean by the Link token being decoupled from the network.

Are you deliberately being obtuse? You're not even addressing my points or logic.

62327991
>>62324959
>the team of paid KOLs whom use 4chan lingo and memes do not post on /biz/ guise!!!!
yeah, sure lil buddy

62328404
test

62328534
>>62327952
>I specifically said CLL accepting payments outside of the Link token and then paying node operators with their team wallets instead of making customers pay in Link or immediately converting the payments to Link from the market decouples the usage of the network from the price.
That's what I said you said.

>miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block
That's completely false.

You're clueless. A clueless ape.

62328546
>>62328404
Testing 1…2…3

62328573
>>62324495
Everything and everyone is going to be flushed in the crypto world and traditional world. And then the new gay system sprouts up from Chainlink. There’s going to be a lot of people out there who made it in other cryptos who then decided to invest their shit like NPC’s into index funds or whatever getting muuh 4% return and are going to be wiped out. The 4% stakers will get isn’t anything remarkable on its face compared to anything else out there except it’s going to be the baseline of the new financial system.

62328670
>>62327991
Maybe he still does, I don't know, I'm saying he's a faggot grifter, other poster (who is obviously adem) was accusing me of being him. Unless you're also him just changing your id, which wouldn't surprise me on this dead board.

62329193
>>62312690
I Sold it all for BTC, I staked some recently, didnt believe in staking before because of wrapping and bridge but with TBV that's can be bypassed. I'm all out of LINK, I hold for years but nothing to show for

62329208
>>62329193
same

62330274
>>62328534
>That's what I said you said.
Devil's in the details; not the same thing and it's why your BTC comparison doesn't work.

>>>miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block
>That's completely false.
Yes anon, there is a consensus for the reward per block. The miners are included in this consensus.

62331087
>>62330274
>not the same thing
It's the same thing.

>there is a consensus for the reward per block. The miners are included in this consensus.
You went from "miners decide" to "miners are part of the decision".

The reality is miners can't decide shit. Even if all miners are 100% in consensus among themselves, all they can do is ask the market to also agree with them. Which is something anyone with a twitter account can do.

62331850
>>62312653
LINK holders are the liberals of crypto, you're completely delusional.
You're on your way out though.

62332143
>>62331087
if miners stop the difficulty plummets
if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
are you legitimately retarded?

62332425
>>62332143
>if miners stop the difficulty plummets
Yes.
And the lower the lower the mining difficulty, the more smaller mining rigs can mine.

>if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
Yes, they'll redirect all their hashrate to a fork nobody will use.

You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.

62332438
All of you need to KYS’sselves

62332450
>>62332425
>miners can't decide shit
>eerrr they can but they won't
I accept your concession, keep moving the goalposts you fucking imbecile

62332473
>>62332450
>>eerrr they can but they won't

kek what the fuck are you talking about?

62332491
glad the chainlink general thread format is finding acceptance

62332578
>>62332491
You keep saying this, but it's pretty much just fuddies creating about 20 fudthreads and then bumping one ad nauseum. The amount of link fud threads on the board doesn't decrease because of this "general" at all. Maybe mods should just ban all LINK fud threads and corral them into a single fuddie general, it's beyond ridiculous now that the board is so slow.

62333330
>>62325222
Chainlink confidential compute for private tokenization too

62335182
>>62332578
shut up cuckold

price + chart :)

62335458
>>62335182
Thanks for bumping /CFG/ (Chainlink Fuddie General)

62336610
>>62332425
>You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.
Still here. Feel free to also refute my point on your BTC comparison falling apart due to the flow of change of hands from new BTC to node operators to market versus new Link tokens going from CLL's uncirculated supply directly to Binance instead of node operators.

62336627
>>62335458
p+c bitch

62336936
>>62316605
>Chainlink pays the nodes from their own team wallets

>>62336610
>Link tokens don't go from CLL's uncirculated supply to node operators

You're a fucking retard you know that.

62337435
>>62333330
Epstein should have stored his blackmail material in here amirite

62337475
If chainlink is decentralized why do not even the biggest, stinkiest linkies here brag about running a node?

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

item.title: Alright fellas, I've been here for more years than I care to admit and although this place is an absolute stinker these… — 44 replies — 35.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Alright fellas, I've been here for more years than I care to admit and although this place is an absolute stinker these…

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-06-09 00:15 UTC

OP

Alright fellas, I've been here for more years than I care to admit and although this place is an absolute stinker these days I'm willing to give it one more change. This market sucks and everyones bored.

Why don't we pick a random ass coin with decent liq and asslow mc just like was once done with SHIB to name one and start shilling here and later x / tg. Everyone's bored and is looking for a quick buck, the market is waiting for something like this.

I've sat through SHIB, APU, HOGE, and various other 200x and still somehow not managed to become a millionaire and I'm getting pretty sick of it. I can't live like this. Let's pick a coin that ticks the boxes and push it as the next SHIB style moon. Let's start by finding the coin, I trust in you.

Early replies (first 3)

62332932
cheese

62332951
This has been tried a hundred times before and it never works out because this place is now filled with tourists and indians who'll fuck it up to lock in their 4 figs of profit

62332953
will never work. 99% of people that make these kinds of threads are part of the industrial token complex that has been fleecing retarded people on here for years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62334378
>>62334203
>for those insane 200x pumps.
god i fuckign kick myself for not selling at Shib's ATH, i could've turned a couple hundred into more than 50k, an actual investment start, but got greedy and just watched it wither away like a retard

62334406
>>62332874
Ya ever think that maybe it’s that piece of fucking shit rugpull scam called toad killer they launched that everyone hates, especially the holders. It would line up with the current market setup

62334451
>>62334406
This
>>62332966
Toad killer is the ultimate shitter you are looking for

62334461
This is a really interesting idea but on deeper reflection I think I'm just going to go buy more memory and cpu stocks instead. Good luck on pumping your worthless meme money though, it sounds like a lot of fun.

62334501
>>62332874
I used to see unironic ghetto niggas with Shibe shirts.

62334505
>>62332874
There's an Epic Fail Guy token on Solana from 2 years ago. It's a microcap but I know this board doesn't like SOL. Perfect situation to repeat what Troll on Solana has done desu. I'll look for some ETH options too.

62334506
>>62332874
What are our options? Personally there’s really only one giant stinky TURD of a coin that comes to mind

62334511
>>62334013
$elien fits all those. my personal thesis is if he ever changes his twitter name to elien musk its gonna pull at least a 10-50x instantly.

hes repeatedly said he's an alien... on rogan's pod
musk: "i keep telling people im an alien but people don't believe me"
rogan: "i believe you"

currently down some lunch money on it, dont care if it goes to zero, reward outweighs the risk imo

62334516
>>62334406
>>62334451
just give up, baggies.
Shyoshi farmed and rugged all of you and he still has 20T more to dump on you.
even Ryoshi gave up on Shitba

F A R M E D

62334517
Toad killer is the official rich pedo token that will make millionaires this upcoming alt season

62334525
people shilling shitters that are years old are tonedeaf to the market. floki is the sole exception to the rule that old tokens never pump. it has to be somewhat new and ideally topical to the cultural zeigeist

and floki of course gave back all the gains after it had its miracle pump back to ath

62334656
Just buy and hold bitcoin, faggot. Stop giving your money away to Indian/eastern european dev scamfaggots

62334676
>>62334516
>Ryoshi gave up on Shitba
Good. Shib is healing.

62334733
>>62334013
MIRAI the WhiteRabbit
>eth
>mc currently under 20k
>clean tokensniff
>cute mascot
>the future will always be in vogue

62335122
>>62334203
The point is we bizbros buy in before the telegrams, x wimps and cuntfluencers do. Just like before, the market conditions are not necessarily a bad thing, in bull you have abundance of shitters now you have nothing really being shilled
>>62334248
Only ETH newfriend
>>62334406
We want something with pref sub 100k mc
>>62334461
It’s all a game and this is the only way to make it in this clown world, your stocks are more rubbish than my morning turd most likely
>>62334733
Name is clumsy and lame. Too little normie appeal with anime. No website.
It needs a website. Other than that not bad keep em coming

62335134
>>62334013
Good list of requirements I would add these:
>must have website and pref some social following
>must be under 100k mc, even as low as 10-20k would be perfect. That’s how you are able to gather momentum fast.

62335164
Sure smells like curry in here.
Have some free gas fee's
>BRAP!

62335171
Thread is gone from the catalog, wrap it up boys.
With that being said, Gondola is the answer.
CMC, Coin Gecko, Etherscan, Dexscreener all paid for and updated.
Website and TG still up and semi active.
Most of the original guys are gone, but a couple are left and keeping it alive.
X premium still being paid for, and the account posts daily.
Just under 100k MC.
Peaked at 12-14M MC, then had its knee caps lopped off when the trump coin BS happened.
Slowly crashed along with the entire market after that.
And unlike what that sol fag posted above, eth coins that make it tend to need a couple years before they really rip.
Gondola is about to hit its 2 year anniversary.

62335469
>>62334511
Also holdin elien on sol for same reason lul. Also down but idc
Actualy giga random that someone wrote elien.. thus it rly has its buyers. I mean when i heard the word instantly went on search as i knew its a Runner

62337794
>>62334511
>>62334733
>>62335171
Will check these ones out. I was also looking at Goblin on eth which recently had a cto because Sam Altman posted about goblins.

62337913
>>62337794
I think gondola or goblin would have the best chance to run unless Elon Musk is actually an alien.
>Gondola
Has the old meme appeal and doesn't really have competition. This is probably the best choice for op's goal out of everything in the thread I'd say.
>Goblin
Has a competitor on SOL which seems to be doing much better. It's about a year old and is trying to shift towards the AI-goblin narrative. Could get a large spike in attention if goblins in ai becomes an actual thing.
>Elien
Hinges on Elon Musk being an alien. Unlikely.
>Mirai
Doesn't have much going for it besides anime girl and low mcap, but is pretty dead without a working website.

I'm leaning towards gondola

62337941
>>62332874
kek shibbagie

62338221
>>62337913
>Hinges on Elon Musk being an alien. Unlikely.

not literally, just depends on how hard he wants to push the meme. theres lots of people that believe themselves to be alien souls despite having explicitly human dna, i think he fits that category. stranger still, theres people that report having contact and conversations with aliens where they confirm such things. some keep this information private because of the social repercussions for reporting on such experiences.

if aliens were a relevant topic in the age of tesla, we could imagine he might think himself as one. his mind so completely foreign to what we know as human.

62338233
>>62332874
How about Nerva/XNV? It's truly decentralized, can only be solo-mined via CPU, and is private. It doesn't match the meme theme of SHIB but I'd argue it's got even more going for it than SHIB did...

62338294
>>62337913
there is also bobo on eth

62338298
MUMU fits the bill.

On ETH
Solid token distribution
Active X and TG
Supported by other 4chan coin communities

0x2F573070E6090b3264Fe707e2C9F201716F123c7

62338308
>>62338294
Mumu & Bobo.

So apparently there is a split between ETH and SOL communities. BOBO and MUMU recently migrated to SOL, leaving the rest of the ETH die hard communities to fend for themselves.

MUMU/ BOBO/ WOJAK/ SNIBBU/ DODO/ APU along with PEPE still have strong communities on ETH.

62338319
>>62338233
Grabbed a bag and already up lol

62338327
>>62338308
>Mumu & Bobo.
The liquidity for Mumu on eth is garbage, but bobo has some strong liquidity. $142k of v2 burned. Snibbu has a decent amount of burned v2 liquidity, too. Same with dodo. Why did they all move to sol?

also op is a fag

62338664
>>62338327
From what I understand, the communities believe the volume for meme coins are on SOL, and Supply control was another factor.

I still think ETH is the home of MEMES and real money.

I've yet to see a buy on any meme coin on sol that made me say "Impressive".

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 97 replies — 19.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 97 • Updated: 2026-06-08 20:26 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62323927
>>62323880
>I am screencapping this whole shit, I have blood in my eyes

62323956
>>62323880

62326531
The falling knife is around $32.8k

Screencap this and thank me later.

62326539
>>62309503
Heavy bag huh?

62326550
>>62311487
ladies and gentlemen

the future of currency

62327899
>>62326531
this guy fucks

62328894
You are missing the most obvious bottom in history. And you are proud of it.

Grim.

62328962
>>62309503
are you dumb or something mate what the fuck the thing went to 58k already

62329036
>>62328962

THIS

op confirmed tard

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

62329970
>>62309503
Dunno m8 at my local groxery store they dib't accept bitcoin. Where xan you buy groceries, other than your cherrypicked example?

62331787
>>62329478
/biz/ always tells you to buy the dip. Then the dip comes, and they tell you not to buy it.

62331939
Brought to you by Epstein and Co.: Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value!

62333686
>>62309503
I bought the rally (my first cycle) and the dips/bottom. waiting on the good days now

62333871
>>62309503
30k

62334072
63K REJECTED

62334365
>>62309503
>65K is the bottom
But it went lower anon?

62334381
>>62329970
you can buy anything you want with a coinbase card, what're you on about geez?

62336135
THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING AT ALL OF THE BEAR SHILLS

YOU LOST. YOU SOLD THE BOTTOM

THE BUYROG WINS AGAIN

BEARSBANE

62336205
>>62309503
At least until it dips again. 26K is the real bottom, screen cap this.

62336221
>>62336205
It is never going 55k. I have no idea why PM metal shills (most of the bear is PM fags, we tested it) watch silver get ended by the Fed, and decide to lash out trolling people's financial lives.

I am glad the BUYROG convinced some people to not miss yet another dip.

62336238
>>62336135
>THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING
>still below the 65k "bottom" of OP

62337880
>>62336238
YOU LOST

item.title: How do you actually cash out? (a large amount) — 26 replies — 10.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do you actually cash out? (a large amount)

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-08 22:53 UTC

OP

I'm planning on selling a decent chunk of my stack next year in order to pay for house construction.

Has anyone sold a large amount OTC and had the funds deposited in a US or European bank account? Any tips?

I'm talking 6 to 7 figures

Early replies (first 3)

62336656
>>62336622
cash out?

62336664
>>62336622
You talk to a tax consultant and let your bank know in advance that you have big crypto deposits coming in and to not flag your account

62336685
>>62336664
thanks, but how do you convert the crypto in the first place? OTC company, crypto exchange, something else?

do you know if some banks are more tolerant than others? i have accounts in the US and Europe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336695
>>62336656
hah, yes but not all of it. i think buying some land and building a house is a pretty good excuse to cash (some) out

62336700
>>62336622
You can’t.

Seriously, new cash outs are only chosen a few times a year, and the minimum requirements for applying are very steep. Even so, hundreds apply, but only a few are selected. If you seriously want to cashour, you’ll likely (but not always) need to have a history on every major exchange that makes you recognizable to the majority of the user-base for several years to even be considered. Pestering the current HODLers or Whales to cashout essentially guarantees that you won’t be selected.

62336708
>>62336622
I pressed withdraw button in Coinbase and typed in amount. Got my bank app notification that the money has arrived within a few minutes

62336710
>>62336700
>minimum requirements for applying are very steep
I'm having trouble understanding your post... are you talking about applying to an OTC desk as a customer?

62336726
>>62336708
are you in the US? and what was the amount, approximately

62336743
>>62336708
I think I just had to notify my bank before hand, but that’s only if you have some ungodly amount being deposited. And that was years ago too, ACH and crypto are pretty common now with banks.

62336763
>>62336726
>>62336743
EU, bank contacted me within a few days. Money was on account already quickly with no problems

62336798
>>62336763
thanks for the response. i'm hoping it's that easy for me.

i would have to open a new coinbase account and transfer btc from some old wallets. i'm just concerned they would have to analyse the old wallet transactions or something. i've just heard of coinbase frequently flagging/freezing accounts and i'm not sure what exactly causes that.

doesn't sound like you had any issues though, which is good.

62336853
>>62336798
I've had the same Coinbase account since 2017 already. This is were the money to crypto first came in and now over the last few years all went out to my bank again.

For tax purposes I've been tracking my crypto with Koinly for the last ~4 years. Maybe about 10 exchanges and 10 wallets, with multiple chains and DeFi trades also. Was not too difficult even. Havent been able to trace everything retroactively perfectly but good enough.

The bank eventually also wanted to have some documents of my transactions. To make sure I am not connected to some blacklisted addresses or that no other money came into my accounts than my own. This is thousands of transactions/buys/sells were talking about. I gave them documents that Koinly can generate. I kind of doubt the bank people can understand it all, but they are satisfied to have something and sense I am not trying to hide anything.

62336859
You don't have enough to sell OTC. You sell to the orderbook.

62336873
>>62336622
SI-SIX SEVEN!!

62336892
>>62336664
Basically this
Unless you're explicitly trying to dodge taxes, which you can't do past cashing out above a certain amount, there's no issues in selling crypto
All the horror stories around here are from dishonest scammers that aren't telling they got their crypto from very poor opsec crime

62336920
Oh yes, absolutely. Because nothing says "carefully planned home construction" quite like tying your entire building budget to one of the most volatile asset classes on Earth and then assuming every intermediary involved will roll out a red carpet while a mountain of money teleports into your bank account.
In this magnificent circus act, the market will obviously cooperate, your chosen exit date will coincide with peak prices, the OTC desk will operate with the speed of a Formula 1 pit crew, your bank's compliance department will greet the incoming wire with confetti cannons, and tax authorities will simply nod approvingly from a distance. What could possibly go wrong?
The fantasy gets even better. You're building a house, a project famous for never experiencing delays, cost overruns, contractor disputes, permit issues, material shortages, or surprise expenses. Pair that with an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment, and you've created the financial equivalent of juggling chainsaws while riding a unicycle across a tightrope made of spaghetti.
Then comes the banking adventure. People talk about large transfers as if they're ordering a pizza. In reality, moving significant sums often involves documentation, source-of-funds checks, compliance reviews, questions, more questions, and occasionally questions about the questions. Yet somehow the expectation is that a giant OTC sale will produce a magical wire transfer that glides through the global financial system like a bald eagle soaring through a sunset.
The best part is the assumption that next year will conveniently be the perfect time to sell. Not six months earlier. Not six months later. Exactly when the concrete needs pouring and the invoices arrive. That's not financial planning; that's consulting a crystal ball while wearing clown shoes.
By all means, have an exit strategy.

62337140
>>62336656
this reminded me of 2017 when crypto was being talked about in the mainstream now and people started asking about whether or not you could actually cash out of crypto lol

62337148
>>62336873
God bless your soul

62337230
>>62336892
the issue will be with the bank that will freeze the account and question you like a criminal. should be resolvable but still a pain in the ass and lets you know who really is the one in control

62337875
>>62336622
the Jews will never allow it

62337902
>>62336920
>an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment
made me chuckle

62337909
>>62336920
funny clanker

62337949
>>62336622
I had huge issues cashing out. Tried to sell on the same exchange where I bought years ago and they wouldn't let me. Beside all the kyc bullshit, had to prove the source of the initial funds, even though they let me buy with no questions asked back then. A nightmare.

62338205
>>62336763
Took out 100k in the summer via Kraken to Revolut. No questions asked at all

62338346
>>62338205
part of making it is triggering the automated unusual activity detections that lead to interrogations or account suspensions on those less caring about individual customers

62338356
>>62336664
I didn't even tell the bank. Just transferred $300K straight from coinbase. They didn't even call like they have for some other large deposits. I did let the accountant know upfront but only because I had to talk with him about some other stuff. If your bank gives you shit about having money then you should get another bank.

item.content:encoded:
Day 625 Of Bobo Rekt in Anus Posting Until BTC is at 180K

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-09 00:59 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62337510

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

item.title: Awesome business idea. Feel free to steal it — 17 replies — 7.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Awesome business idea. Feel free to steal it

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-08 23:17 UTC

OP

The IRL Minecraft Club

The IRL Minecraft Club is a membership-based outdoor recreation community where people participate in a real-world sandbox experience inspired by the creativity and freedom of Minecraft.

Members gain access to a large shared property designed to function as a living, evolving world. The land includes forests, farmland, water sources, workshops, gathering areas, and designated resource extraction zones. Within established safety and environmental guidelines, members are free to shape the landscape, build structures, cultivate crops, create projects, and collaborate with other members.

Core Activities

* Building structures and settlements
* Farming and food production
* Forestry and resource harvesting
* Quarrying and stone work
* Crafting and engineering projects
* Community events and challenges
* Trade and cooperation between members

The goal is to create a persistent world that develops over time through the collective efforts of its members.

Membership Model

Revenue is generated through recurring memberships that provide access to the property, shared tools, equipment, resources, and organized activities.

Members can participate casually for a day of outdoor recreation or become long-term contributors who help shape the evolving community.

Early replies (first 3)

62338009
Food and Commerce

An on-site food vendor provides meals, snacks, and supplies. Members may also bring their own food or produce food through approved farming and agricultural activities within the club.

Safety and Stewardship

Staff members oversee site operations, maintain equipment, monitor safety practices, care for animals, and enforce community standards. While participants accept inherent risks through membership agreements and waivers, the club maintains reasonable safety procedures and operational oversight.

Community Rules

The club operates under a code of conduct designed to encourage cooperation, respect, and responsible land stewardship. Members who violate these standards may lose access to the community.

Vision

The IRL Minecraft Club aims to become a modern “third place”—a social environment outside of home and work where people can gather, create, learn practical skills, spend time outdoors, and build something meaningful together.

Rather than consuming entertainment, members actively participate in creating the world around them. Every structure, farm, pathway, and project becomes part of a living community built by the people who use it.

62338016
Sorry For using AI but it is a better writer than me. Isn’t this a genius as hell idea

62338034
>>62338006
So you're going to charge people a subscription to camp in the same spot, expect them to (poorly) build the amenities, and find someone willing to insure this?
You know what though OP, maybe we can convince the new generation to be contributing members of society by rebranding life as some sort of Minecraft game. They pay a subscription to the IRS and they get to love in a world where they can try and find a place to live and not get eaten by zombies?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62338049
>>62338016
>* Farming and food production
>* Forestry and resource harvesting
>* Quarrying and stone work
Congrats, you just described the fucking economy. If you want to do this without investing 50B in machinery and bribes for politicians, you got competition in the Amish. But you can try to rebrand the religious cult for social media braindead younglings.

62338076
>>62338049
>50 billion in machinery

Only simple tools. Pick axes, shovels, axes, etc, to start. As the club grows and evolves they can vote on investments such as machinery as a -portion of the profits get reinvested into the club.

62338086
>>62338006
Have you ever tried digging a 1 meter by 1 meter hole in dirt?

62338097
>>62338006
It's a good idea if you are handsome and people like you (are you over 6ft?)
otherwise finding land will be impossible. There are however municipalities with a lot of "conservation" land that they don't have any way of improving or utilizing even in environmentally and "socially positive" ways.

Basically if you live in a nice area with public land that is under utilized, yes, you're minecraft club could get support from local governments.
It relies entirely on your IRL level of charm, and your local government. Or find a rich person who has land.

62338099
>>62338006
build 5x5x5 (meter) house (just floor and ceiling and support columns in corners)

manages to rise the 5m structure somehow from dirt or planks
invites frens on the roof to admire it all
redecorates/upgrades to bricks: removes columns #1 and #2
guys can you help me with this work?
uh guys?
guys?

62338104
>>62338097
>Basically if you live in a nice area with public land that is under utilized, yes, you're minecraft club could get support from local governments.
build the nice treehouse
nails down floor for it to be safe
people playing 6767 moving things a bit, upgrading
go to other part of house
step on exposed nail
112 hello

62338130
>>62338097
Somebody else will have to pull it off. I am autistic, I can’t charm my local governments into supporting the club. I am 6’2’’ though and muscular

62338150
>>62338130
>I am 6’2’’ though and muscular
honestly that is probably sufficient, I say go for it bro.

62338249
>>62338006
OP remembered the movie Downsizing

62338311
>>62338006
Can't help but chuckle that you're describing a labor camp (or the real life economy, lmao). But there is probably something to it. Something like renting outdoor spaces to do that Primitive Technology thing that that youtube guy does.

62338330
Its a neat idea but you would need to be really close to a major city to get interest and if you own a good amount of land near a major city you are likely already too rich to need a business ideas.

62338357
Nah that's dumb. Normies (aka: families - your only viable target market) will not want to do this. Better to use the land to plant strawberries/blueberries and charge people to come pick the berries.

62338364
>>62338357
me? I would plant cotton and put my farm near atlanta

62338435
>>62338006
>blablabla rules blabla
>membership fee blabla
Sounds like some shitty reddit polycule.
